response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer with broad polyglot experience, but he is a poor match for this role on every mandatory dimension. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, far below the 5–10 years required; his primary stack is JavaScript/TypeScript with Node.js and MongoDB. There is no evidence of Windows platform development, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge—the adjacent RIDE Capital role was focused on general investment tooling using Node and GraphQL, not financial-product systems.
strengths: Demonstrates solid CI/CD and DevOps practices (GitLab CI, Docker, trunk-based development) which aligns with the optional desirable skill
Java experience (robotic arms and mobile orchestration at Altom) partially satisfies the optional Java background criterion
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record evidenced by workshops, public talks, and open-source projects
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus one thesis project), massively short of the 5–10 year mandatory requirement
Zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or investment banking systems—neither domain track satisfies the role's requirement
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database background is MongoDB and generic SQL, with no evidence of enterprise RDBMS work on Windows
